source_jobs.title: Talent Consultant
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/92a582e8-583a-47df-a313-195ee9e5d403/62369b87-e436-4447-827a-9c074897ee20/Logo%201.7x1.png]



Talent Safari - Talent Associate

Location: Remote (African Timezone).

Type: Full‑time




MISSION

Talent Safari helps African startups hire great talent. Demand from our clients is growing rapidly; we need a sharp operator to source great talent, reliably vet them, and keep clients satisfied.

We’re looking for someone young, smart, hardworking, ready-to-learn and with some prior experience in the startup space.

Since launching in January 2025 we have become the go-to talent partner for innovative businesses across Africa, working with clients such as Paystack, Peach Payments and Turaco.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own searches from kick‑off to close: spec, sourcing, screening, offer, onboarding.

 - Work on a range or roles from some of Africa’s most exciting companies

 - Manage 5‑10 client accounts; push back, problem‑solve, close roles fast.

 - Run targeted outreach via LinkedIn, the Talent Safari community, referrals, and AI tools.

 - Input to processes, suggest improvements, help us to grow and optimize.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2–5 years of total experience, ideally in startup recruitment. We’ll also consider high‑output ops/strategy talent from fast‑moving startups.

 - Evidence you learn fast and execute faster—shipped projects, closed hires, or hustles.

 - Clear, concise written & spoken English.

 - Appetite for high-intensity work, blunt feedback and autonomy.


BONUS POINTS

 - French proficiency.

 - Technical background or experience hiring tech roles (engineering, product, data).

 - Strong network/experience in African startups.

 - Experience working in South Africa


PRACTICALITIES

We are open to candidates based anywhere who are able to work on Africa timezone. However, we are particularly keen on candidates in startup hubs such as Nairobi, Cape Town, Lagos, Abidjan, Kigali, etc.

If you are based in Nairobi then you will be expected to work one-day a week in-person with the Nairobi team




WHY JOIN

Work directly with founders at Africa's leading startups alongside a small, fast-moving team with incredibly high standards.

source_jobs.title: Join the Talent Safari Community
source_jobs.description:
Talent Safari helps innovative companies across Africa find great talent for their teams.

Complete this application to join our talent community. You will then be considered first for future opportunities that Talent Safari is hiring for.

source_jobs.title: Founders Scouting Lead
source_jobs.description:
BACKGROUND

Allan & Gill Gray Philanthropy Rwanda (AGGPR) has embarked on a journey to develop responsible entrepreneurs across the African continent in the service of the common good.

Our Vision is an empowered, prosperous, productively engaged African citizenry thriving in ethical societies with dignity and hope.

Our Mission is to attack poverty by investing in, nurturing, and empowering responsible entrepreneurs and leaders who benefit society by helping to accelerate meaningful employment creation, while embodying the values and behaviors of ethical leadership.

Our Values are long-term commitment, spirit of humility and significance, excellence, responsible stewards, and diversity and inclusion.

Jasiri, one of AGGPR’s main programs, invests in, nurtures, and empowers entrepreneurs who benefit society and attack poverty through the creation of high-impact new businesses, creating new markets on the African continent.

Jasiri Talent Investor is a one-year program for high-potential individuals who display exceptional entrepreneurial potential to form part of a community of high-impact, responsible entrepreneurs growing businesses from scratch. Participants are funded and guided through a process of team formation, idea generation, business validation, and market entry. The program introduces Jasiri Fellows to equally ambitious co-founders and supports them on an individual, team, and business level through seasoned Facilitators and Coaches.

Jasiri Growth Accelerator invests in early stages, high-potential startups that may still be too risky for commercial funders to invest in. Early-stage ventures with potential for high growth and demonstrated product-market fit are provided with funding to support their growth and assisted with investor readiness.

Wavumbuzi – a Jasiri pipeline building program – is a free annual six-week online entrepreneurship challenge focused on developing the entrepreneurial mindset of secondary/high school learners throughout the country. The challenge is designed to equip students with competencies to be the next generation of global leaders, change-makers, and innovative thinkers.




ABOUT THE POSITION

The Founders Scouting Lead is a pivotal leadership role within Jasiri, designed to shape and execute a forward-looking Founders sourcing strategy that fuels the organization’s mission to build commercially viable, high-growth, high-impact ventures across Africa. This role is responsible for positioning Jasiri’s competitive edge and implementing intelligence-driven headhunting and ecosystem engagement.

The Founders Scouting Lead will lead the design and implementation of targeted scouting strategies to proactively identify, attract, and convert exceptional entrepreneurial and technical talent for Jasiri Programs. This includes sourcing repeat founders, visionary product developers, technical experts, and operators who can drive venture success.

This role reports to the East Africa Regional Director and will supervise the Recruitment Officers and Headhunters across Rwanda, Kenya, Ethiopia & South Africa.




KEY ROLES & RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and execute a robust Founders Scouting Strategy across our countries of operations, to identify and attract exceptional entrepreneurial and technical talent meeting our eligibility criteria/founder profile.

 - Spearhead proactive headhunting initiatives and recruitment campaigns, leveraging strategic networks. Ensure campaigns are data-driven, context-specific, and aligned with evolving program objectives.

 - Build and maintain a dynamic talent intelligence system to monitor pipeline health, analyse candidate quality, and identify gaps. Use insights from selection outputs and data analytics to continuously refine recruitment strategies and improve application quality across countries.

 - Initiate and manage high-value partnerships with key stakeholders (governments, entrepreneurship ecosystems, global leadership programs, investors, diaspora netw

source_jobs.title: Information Security GRC Officer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAYSTACK

Paystack’s mission is to help merchants in Africa get paid by anyone, anywhere in the world. Tens of thousands of notable corporations and businesses in Africa use Paystack’s modern payments infrastructure, including MTN, Bolt, Domino’s Pizza, Smile Communications, AXA Mansard Insurance, and many others.

Within over 9 years, our close-knit team has introduced a steady cadence of innovations within the African Payment space, such as automated recurring payments, the ability for customers to pay with only a bank account, automated chargebacks, and much more. We process well over $300 million in transactions monthly, and our double-digit growth has us processing even more every month.

We’ve pioneered a new era of simple, powerful technology tools for businesses in Africa, and as we prepare for a period of accelerated growth, we’re looking to you to help build a solid foundation upon which we can support some of the continent’s best-performing businesses.

At Paystack, we believe that we will win by recruiting talented people and giving them the space, resources, and support to do the best work of their lives. We hire the most capable people, treat them with genuine respect, and give them the means to succeed. And we’d love your help!

As a leader of the Information Security function at Paystack, you will contribute to the business by presenting your technical expertise with a balanced approach to communication and a bias for action. You will be required to work with our leadership teams at all levels of the company to drive solutions that impact our platform for the good of Paystack and ultimately, our customers and stakeholders. You will leverage your experience across various tools and processes to establish policies and standards across the company.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

As a member of the security team, you will contribute to the Security Organization by presenting your technical expertise with a balanced approach to communication and a bias for action. You will be required to work with a team of information security professionals, other teams within Paystack, and Paystack's customers to map out and effect change across Paystack’s information security landscape. You will leverage your experience across various tools and processes to establish policies and standards across the company.




JOB RESPONSIBILITIES

The responsibilities of a security analyst (GRC) at Paystack include:

 - Contributing to establishing strategic information security objectives across Paystack.

 - Contributing to the strategic direction for Security Governance, Risk Management, and Compliance that aligns with the overarching Security objectives of the company

 - Understanding the unique challenges of securing the Paystack platform across different markets and demographics

 - Identifying control gaps and testing the design of existing controls

 - Determining risk management controls and recommending improvements to company-wide controls

 - Ability to work effectively with a team to execute various security projects, evaluate controls, and plan around solutions

 - Ability to communicate effectively

 - Ability to own and manage portions of the security program and provide consistent status updates to Security Leadership regarding progress against objectives

 - Raise accountability by escalating issues in a timely manner and creating and maintaining detailed documentation

 - Stay up-to-date with trends in the information security community

 - Operate with a sense of ownership, urgency, and drive

 - Ability to distill controls across multiple regulatory requirements and frameworks for visibility into defence mechanisms, strengths, and gaps

 - Contribute to the development of our Internal vendor risk management program; this involves working with vendors and partners to ensure they have appropriate controls in place

 - Documenting exceptions to establish security policies, guidelines, and standards; ensuring exceptions are revie

source_jobs.title: Chief Technology Officer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Fixa is building the HR and financial infrastructure for Africa’s informal and blue-collar workforce. Our platform supports businesses in construction, manufacturing, agriculture, and other labor-intensive sectors with powerful tools for workforce management, payroll, payments, and financial services.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a CTO to build Fixa's engineering function from founding-team scale to Series A — and ideally Series B — scale of 100,000+ users. Personally hands-on through the first 12 months, in the weeds solving problems alongside the team, with the judgment to know when to shift to a leadership-heavy posture. Anchor the architectural decisions that compound into scalable infrastructure and multi-country rollout — starting with a stable HR platform, expanding through banking and payment rails, and laying the foundation for Fixa to become a fintech institution.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

Outcomes (12–18 months)

 1. Strong product reliability — driving customer-reported defects toward zero.
    
    - Customer-reported bug count tracked quantitatively, trending sharply downward month over month.
    
    - Reworked code rate tracked as a primary code-health metric, trending down.
    
    - Code review enforced. Automated test coverage thresholds in place.
    
    - SLOs are defined and actively enforced against the existing observability stack

 2. Multi-tenant architecture evolution and multi-country scale.
    
    - Own the architectural decision and migration path from per-tenant MySQL toward row-level or hybrid multi-tenancy as customer count scales. Core to the acumen and responsibility of the role.
    
    - Ensure infrastructure can support the migration without a stop-the-world rewrite.
    
    - Architect the Country layer for cross-border expansion.

 3. API platform and partner integrations.
    
    - Architect and ship internal APIs at production quality — specifically enabling Fixa to build and launch a credit scoring API as a product offering.
    
    - Integrate with external partner APIs seamlessly across payment rails, KYC, banking, and identity.
    
    - Build the abstraction layer, so adding a new country or provider is a plug-in, not a rewrite.

 4. Data infrastructure and data quality.
    
    - Clean, trustworthy data across production and analytics, on infrastructure built to scale.
    
    - Articulated, executable data plan — covering ingestion, storage, modeling, quality controls, and partner data exchange. (Candidates will be asked to present a draft data plan in the Phase 3 technical assessment.)

 5. Lean, AI-empowered engineering team built and operational.
    
    - Personally own assessment, sourcing, interviewing, and building of the founding engineering team.
    
    - Productivity measurement systems live — output metrics, code health, and engineer effectiveness. Input methods and guardrails are defined upfront.




WHAT YOU HAVE

Must Haves

 - 7+ years of professional software engineering, predominantly in JavaScript/Node.js or TypeScript as primary backend language.

 - Must have 3+ years of experience in startup or scaleup environments & 4+ years in upper engineering management

 - Must have Fintech transaction systems experience at scale. You have built or materially contributed to systems processing hundreds of thousands of transactions.

 - Previously shipped production systems on AWS (Lambda / API Gateway serverless patterns strongly preferred).

 - Worked with MySQL or comparable relational DBs at multi-tenant scale.

 - Must have Frontend depth in React

 - Must have awareness and architectural understanding of Flutter or a comparable mobile framework
   
   - Hands-on Flutter experience is a plus

Strong Preferences

 - API architecture and integration depth. You have built APIs consumed at production scale; has integrated multiple third-party providers in production.

 - Platform architecture — multi-tenancy and AWS serverless. Pro

source_jobs.title: General Manager, Africa
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

AltSchool Africa is a pan-African online school built for the future of work. Founded in Lagos in 2021, the company trains non-technical and semi-technical Africans in high-demand skills, such as software engineering, data, product, business analytics, and the creative economy. It partners with accredited higher institutions to award diploma certificates. The platform is fully virtual, hardware-agnostic, and designed for learners across the continent regardless of socioeconomic background.



AltSchool's model sits at the intersection of education and employability: learners gain globally competitive skills, and the company connects them to opportunities. Its long-term goal is to equip 10 million Africans with profitable, in-demand skills by 2030.



What AltSchool Africa Does

 - Delivers structured, diploma-accredited online programmes in tech, business, data, and creative skills.

 - Partners with higher institutions to provide recognized credentials alongside practical skill training.

 - Operates a flexible, affordable pricing model ($20-$50/month) to reduce barriers to entry.

 - Supports learners from 76+ countries with content and community built for African contexts.

 - Maintains a country-level presence in Nigeria and Kenya, with plans for broader expansion.



Traction & Market Context

 - 20,000+ enrolled students across Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, Rwanda, and the UK as of late 2023.

 - Kenya launched as the second-largest revenue market in January 2024; Swahili-language content is in development.

 - $1M pre-seed raised in 2022; Seed round of $50K at $11M valuation led by Angaza and C-One.

 - Revenue growth trajectory signals 10x potential with the right operational leadership in place.



The Mandate

Africa's youth population is the world's fastest-growing. By 2050, one in four people globally will be African, yet the continent faces a structural skills mismatch: millions of job seekers lack the in-demand digital and business skills required for the modern economy. AltSchool directly addresses this gap at scale, combining affordable access, accredited credentials, and pan-African reach.

The company has proven demand and early revenue. It is now seeking a General Manager to work alongside the founding team, an operational leader who can hold the business accountable to its execution plans, build institutional and funder partnerships, and drive AltSchool into its next phase of growth. This is the mandate we have been engaged to fill




ABOUT THE ROLE

The General Manager will serve as AltSchool Africa's chief operating leader, working directly alongside the founding team to translate vision into execution. This is a builder's role, not a manager of a steady-state business. The GM will own the company's day-to-day operations, revenue growth, institutional partnerships, and team accountability, while owning fundraising and external stakeholder relationships.

This role is well-suited to a seasoned African operator who combines strong commercial instincts with a genuine commitment to education and impact and has the credibility and network to open doors with institutions, governments, and impact-focused funders across East and West Africa.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Own operational execution — translate founder strategy into measurable quarterly and annual plans, track progress, and hold the team accountable.

 - Lead revenue growth — develop and execute go-to-market strategies across Nigeria, Kenya, and new markets; drive student acquisition, retention, and lifetime value.

 - Build and manage institutional partnerships — universities, government agencies, employers, and sector bodies across East and West Africa.

 - Support fundraising — identify, cultivate, and convert relationships with impact investors, development finance institutions, foundations, and grant-making bodies.

 - Lead cross-functional teams — oversee growth, business development, and operations functions; build team capability and

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAYSTACK

Paystack’s mission is to accelerate digital payments on the continent of Africa. Over 200,000 of some of the most renowned businesses in Nigeria, as well as a growing number of merchants in South Africa, Ghana, and Kenya, use Paystack’s modern payments gateway. They include the likes of MTN, Burger King, UPS, AXA Mansard Insurance, FairMoney, PiggyVest, FilmHouse Cinemas, Smile Communications, Air Peace Airlines, Crocs, Under Armour, Richemont Lifestyle Group (RLG), and many others.

In just over 10 years, our growing team has introduced a steady cadence of innovation within the online payments landscape, such as automated recurring payments, the ability for customers to pay directly from their bank accounts, automated chargebacks, and much more.

We’re obsessed with building the best place to work on the continent while accelerating digital commerce across Africa, and we need your help!




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a senior full-stack engineer, you take responsibility for delivering and supporting robust back-end and front-end applications that expose our data and functionality to internal and external users.

Applications handling financial information need to be able to handle large data sets in a performant manner, while being highly accurate and reliable.

You’ll work with a team of talented engineers to design and build systems that support some of South Africa’s best-known brands.



WHAT WE EXPECT OF YOU:

 - Architect and Developer: Design and implement robust solutions for our platform, ensuring scalability, efficiency, and reliability.

 - Product Innovation: Design and development of technical solutions to deliver on our product vision.

 - Technical Problem Solving: Tackle complex technical challenges, providing innovative solutions to enhance system performance and reliability.

 - System Integration: Collaborate on the refinement of the platform’s architecture, focusing on optimization and seamless integration to other systems.

 - Code Review and Testing: Review specifications and code submissions and conduct thorough testing to maintain code quality.

 - System support: Collaborate with other teams, both inside and outside engineering, to assist merchants and customers where necessary.




WHAT YOU ARE GOOD AT:

 - You have extensive programming experience in at least one language, such as JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, C++, C#, Python, or another major language

 - Working on large, distributed systems at scale

 - Understanding and working with SQL databases

 - Writing solid unit testing and integration tests

 - You communicate clearly and are able to collaborate in a remote environment

 - Taking ownership of the work assigned to you

 - You have a passion for building products to help African businesses




TECHNOLOGY STACK:

Our technology stack includes the following:

 - Typescript and JavaScript

 - Node.js

 - Frameworks: Express.js, Sails.js, and Nest.js

 - Frontend frameworks: React, Angular, and Vue

 - Redis

 - MySQL and MongoDB

 - Docker

 - Kubernetes

 - AWS

source_jobs.title: Financial Crime Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAYSTACK

Over the past nine years, Paystack has established itself as a pioneer in African fintech with a mission to help merchants get paid by anyone, anywhere in the world. Processing over $300 million in monthly transactions, our modern payments infrastructure supports tens of thousands of notable corporations, including MTN, Bolt, and Domino’s Pizza. As Paystack enters a phase of accelerated growth, we are seeking a Financial Crime Manager to own, design, and scale our fraud and AML detection capabilities. This role sits within the Compliance Operations team, carrying end-to-end accountability for ensuring our financial crime processes are robust, domain-accurate, and scalable across multiple markets. This is a hands-on leadership role where you will define operational strategies, guide system design, and directly influence how financial crime risk is managed at Paystack while leading and developing high-performing fraud and AML teams.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

As the Financial Crime Manager, you will lead our fraud and AML detection operations from end-to-end. You will partner with product and engineering to evolve system capabilities while leading a high-performing team of specialists to deliver high-quality risk outcomes. By combining deep domain expertise with operational leadership, you will ensure Paystack remains a safe and trusted payments platform.

You will be accountable for:

 - Informing the product roadmap for our monitoring, alerting, and case management tools.

 - The quality and effectiveness of fraud and AML monitoring logic and detection scenarios.

 - The operating model and performance of the Financial Crime Monitoring teams

 - Translating risk, regulatory, and business requirements into robust, scalable detection systems

 - Driving operational excellence by using data and metrics to manage team capacity and performance.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 1. Financial Crime Oversight
    
    - Own the end-to-end fraud and AML detection domain, ensuring alignment between prevention, detection, investigation, and remediation.
    
    - Apply deep understanding of fraud typologies, AML/CTF risks, sanctions, and regulatory expectations to detection design.
    
    - Manage the Fraud and AML operational teams (specialists and first-line managers) to ensure adequate coverage, capability and day-to-day execution.
    
    - Translate regulatory, partner, and audit requirements into scalable technical and operational controls.
    
    - Stay ahead of evolving financial crime patterns, market-specific risks, and regulatory developments across Paystack’s footprint.

 2. Financial Crime Capabilities
    
    - Design and optimise systems, logic, scenarios, and thresholds used to identify financial crime.
    
    - Ensure adequate coverage and capability across the team to meet growing transaction volumes.
    
    - Apply a deep understanding of fraud typologies, AML/CTF risks, and sanctions to the design of our controls.
    
    - Establish feedback loops between investigation outcomes and detection strategies to improve signal quality.
    
    - Maintain auditable documentation of detection logic, business assumptions, and strategy changes.
    
    - Measure and manage detection performance using quantitative metrics (precision, recall, false positives, alert-to-case conversion, loss metrics).

 3. Team Leadership & Development
    
    - Manage and mentor the Fraud and AML operational teams, including specialists and first-line managers.
    
    - Set clear direction, provide coaching, and oversee performance management to ensure high-quality execution.

 4. Operational Excellence
    
    - Build and improve SLAs, KPIs, and quality frameworks across all Financial Crime teams.
    
    - Use performance metrics to manage team capacity and ensure efficient investigation outcomes.
    
    - Balance speed, quality, and regulatory expectations with a focus on maintaining a positive customer experience.

 5. C

source_jobs.title: PHP Developer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Mogo is Kenya's fast-scaling fintech - part of Eleving Group, a global financial services platform operating across 16+ countries. We are redefining access to vehicle financing, microfinance, and smartphone financing for everyday Kenyans. Our mission is simple: make progress accessible.

We're growing fast, and we need the people infrastructure to match. That's where you come in.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a skilled PHP Developer to join our engineering team. In this role, you will play a crucial part in building and scaling software solutions tailored for the fintech and lending sector. You will work on robust, modern PHP codebases, ensuring high performance, reliability, and security. This is an excellent opportunity for a developer who thrives in a collaborative environment, values clean architectural design, and wants to see their work have a direct impact on financial technology solutions.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Develop & Maintain: Write clean, well-designed, and efficient PHP code using modern frameworks to support our core fintech and lending products.

 - Architect & Design: Utilize good object-oriented design skills and software architecture paradigms to build scalable and maintainable features.

 - Test & Quality Assurance: Ensure code quality and stability by writing comprehensive unit tests using PHPUnit.

 - Deploy & Optimize: Work closely with modern development workflows, utilizing basic CI/CD pipelines, Docker, and Kubernetes for containerized deployments.

 - Collaborate: Participate in sprint planning, task tracking, and documentation within Atlassian Jira, and collaborate effectively with team members in English.




WHAT YOU HAVE

Must-Haves (Required)

 - Experience: 3+ years of professional working experience as a PHP developer.

 - PHP Expertise: Strong, hands-on experience with PHP 8.1 or higher.

 - Frameworks: Solid experience with at least one major PHP framework, specifically Symfony or Zend (Laminas).

 - Domain Knowledge: Prior experience developing software for fintech, lending companies, or a highly similar domain.

 - Testing: Proven experience writing Unit tests and a solid familiarity with PHPUnit.

 - Tools & Environment: Proficiency with Git for version control.
   
   - Basic command line (terminal) usage.
   
   - Familiarity with issue-tracking software, specifically Atlassian Jira.

 - Architecture & DevOps: Good object-oriented design skills and an understanding of software architecture patterns.
   
   - Basic understanding of CI/CD concepts and best practices.
   
   - Practical experience and knowledge of Docker and Kubernetes (K8s).

 - Language: Proficiency in English, both verbal and written.



Preferred

 - Database Skills: Strong general SQL knowledge.

 - PostgreSQL: Hands-on experience with PostgreSQL, including query optimization for high-performance applications.




WHO YOU ARE

 - A Quality-Focused Developer: You don't just write code that works; you write code that lasts, backed by solid unit testing and architectural patterns.

 - A Problem Solver: You understand the nuances and security demands of financial software and enjoy tackling complex logical challenges.

 - A Team Player: You communicate clearly, document your progress in Jira, and collaborate smoothly across technical boundaries.

source_jobs.title: Debt Collection Process Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Mogo is Kenya's fast-scaling fintech - part of Eleving Group, a global financial services platform operating across 16+ countries. We are redefining access to vehicle financing, microfinance, and smartphone financing for everyday Kenyans. Our mission is simple: make progress accessible.

We are dedicated to fostering upward social mobility in diverse communities by providing credit access to entrepreneurs who are often excluded from traditional banking. Through a combination of asset financing, fintech innovations, AI-based underwriting, and proprietary car portals, we empower local entrepreneurs, such as motorcycle taxi riders, Uber/Bolt drivers, and small business owners, to purchase their own vehicles and obtain loans to expand their businesses.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a data-driven and hands-on Debt Collection Process Lead to design, optimize, and manage end-to-end collections workflows for vehicle and device finance in Kenya.

In this role, you will lead initiatives that strengthen the customer repayment experience while ensuring collections operations remain efficient, compliant, and scalable. On a day-to-day basis, you will work closely with the Head of Debt Collection and partner teams across Credit/Risk, Customer Service, Product/IT, and Legal to turn data insights into operational improvements.

This is a high-impact role for someone who is proactive, structured, and takes full ownership.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 1. Process design & optimization
    
    - Design and continuously improve consumer collections processes across all delinquency stages
    
    - Introduce and scale automation (case allocation rules, workflows, customer communications) and explore robotics/AI use cases to improve efficiency and cost-to-collect.
    
    - Audit current workflows, improve them end-to-end (test and implement changes), and design new processes where needed.

 2. Documentation & training
    
    - Create and maintain SOPs, playbooks, and training materials to ensure consistent execution across internal teams and external partners (if applicable).
    
    - Support onboarding and continuous training for collections agents and related stakeholders.

 3. Cross-functional delivery
    
    - Partner with Customer Service, Credit/Risk, Product/IT, Legal, and any BPO/agency partners to implement data-driven collections improvements.
    
    - Coordinate testing and rollouts of new strategies, communications, tooling changes, and policy updates.

 4. Continuous improvement
    
    - Use customer feedback, agent input, and performance analytics to identify bottlenecks (e.g., contractability, broken PTP, queue management, dispute handling).
    
    - Propose improvements, drive execution, and track impact through clear initiatives and timelines.

 5. Compliance & governance
    
    - Ensure processes, scripts, and customer communication standards meet local regulatory requirements and internal policies.
    
    - Maintain alignment with consumer protection principles, complaint handling expectations, and audit readiness.

 6. Performance measurement & reporting
    
    - Monitor performance metrics and initiative results using tools such as Power BI and SQL (or equivalent reporting systems).
    
    - Communicate outcomes and next steps clearly to stakeholders, including regular updates to the Head of Debt Collection.




WHAT YOU HAVE

Must-Haves (Required)

 - Experience in improving operational processes

 - Comfortable working with data and dashboards to understand performance and track results (strong Excel skills).

 - Ability to work with different teams and get things done together (Customer Service, Credit/Risk, Product/IT, Legal, and external partners when needed).

 - Strong sense of ownership, with good communication and stakeholder management skills.

 - Willingness to relocate to Nairobi (Kenya).

Preferred

 - Experience with debt collection processes.

 - Interest in automation

source_jobs.title: Head of Sales (Device Financing)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Mogo is Kenya's fast-scaling fintech - part of Eleving Group, a global financial services platform operating across 16+ countries. We are dedicated to fostering upward social mobility in diverse communities by providing credit access to entrepreneurs who are often excluded from traditional banking.

Through a combination of asset financing, fintech innovations, AI-based underwriting, and proprietary car portals, we empower local entrepreneurs, such as motorcycle taxi riders, Uber/Bolt drivers, and small business owners, to purchase their own vehicles and obtain loans to expand their businesses.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

Mogo Kenya has successfully launched its device financing business and is now seeking a strategic, results-driven Head of Sales (Device Financing) to take ownership of the sales function and scale it to the next level. This role is focused on driving smartphone and device sales performance - expanding the dealer and retail network, accelerating customer acquisition, and turning Mogo’s device financing into a market-leading product in Kenya.

 

The Head of Sales (Device Financing) will be accountable for the commercial performance of the device financing program, leading the sales organization, optimizing the go-to-market model, and delivering aggressive growth targets.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

1. Strategic Leadership:

 - Develop and implement the device financing strategy, aligning with overall business objectives.

 - Identify and analyze market trends and opportunities in the device financing space.

 - Set performance targets and KPIs for the device financing program.

2. Program Management:

 - Oversee the end-to-end device financing process, from product development to customer onboarding and repayment.

 - Manage relationships with financing partners, including banks, lenders, and technology providers.

 - Ensure compliance with relevant regulations and industry standards.

3. Team Leadership:

 - Lead and motivate a team of device financing professionals, providing guidance and support.

 - Build a high-performing team with the necessary skills and expertise.

 - Conduct performance reviews and provide feedback to team members.

4. Financial Performance:

 - Monitor the financial performance of the device financing program.

 - Identify areas for improvement and implement strategies to enhance profitability.

 - Prepare financial reports and presentations for senior management.

5. Product Development:

 - Identify opportunities for product innovation and enhancements.

 - Partner with product teams to develop and launch new device financing products and features.

 - Conduct market research and analyze customer feedback to inform product development decisions




WHAT YOU HAVE

Must-Haves (Required)

 - 5+ years of experience in sales leadership roles within fintech, consumer finance, telecom, device financing, retail, or related industries.

 - Demonstrated experience leading and scaling sales teams in fast-growth or emerging-market environments.

 - Strong background in building and managing dealer, distributor, or retail partner networks.

 - Solid commercial acumen with the ability to interpret financial data and translate it into actionable sales strategies.

 - Excellent leadership and people management skills, including experience managing mid-level managers and large field-based sales teams.

 - Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.

 - Ability to balance strategic vision with hands-on execution.

 - Strong stakeholder management and cross-functional collaboration skills.

 - University degree in Business, Finance, Marketing, Economics, or a related field; MBA or equivalent is a plus.

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English.

Nice to have (Preferred)

 - Experience in device financing, BNPL (Buy Now Pay Later), mobile money, or telecom-related sales.

 - Familiarity with the Kenyan smartphone and retail ecosystem.

 - Prior experien

source_jobs.title: Financial Controller
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

We are a mission-driven platform transforming higher education access for students and working professionals across Sub-Saharan Africa. We believe that quality education should be a universal right, not a privilege.

Our platform has already guided thousands of learners by connecting them to hundreds of world-class institutions and study destinations globally, unlocking their potential and ensuring every individual has the tools




ABOUT THE ROLE

Our firm is a fast-growing organization scaling rapidly across multiple countries. As we scale, weak financial controls become expensive fast — late collections strangle cash flow, mispaid vendors damage relationships, and disorganized books make every decision harder. This role exists to make sure none of that happens.

You'll oversee two full-time accounting team members who handle the hands-on work: making payments, recording bookkeeping entries, following up with customers, and reconciling payments as they come in. Because they own the day-to-day, your job is oversight, not execution — you own the outcomes. On the AR side, that means collections happen on time, and you step in on the hard cases. On the AP side, that means vendors are billed, verified, and paid correctly and on time. Underneath both, you review their bookkeeping so the books stay accurate and current. On a normal day, this is a couple of focused reviews, not hours of data entry — the team handles the volume.

Beyond finance, you'll handle key administrative work across the company, legal, and HR — the operational backbone that keeps a multi-country company compliant and running.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Oversee collections. Make sure AR is invoiced and chased on schedule, and step in on the difficult or escalated cases to get them closed. Day-to-day chasing is the AR officer's job; you handle exceptions.

 - Oversee payables. Ensure vendors and obligations are billed, validated, and paid correctly and on time, escalating anything irregular before it becomes a problem. Payment authorization above a set threshold sits with the finance manager or a co-founder.

 - Review the day-to-day bookkeeping done by the junior accountants — catching errors, enforcing consistency, and keeping the books clean and current.

 - Own the month-end close, and work with the Finance Manager to produce management reports and board packs.

 - Own our audits — preparing for them, managing the process, and seeing them through to completion.

 - Own tax compliance across our markets — making sure filings and obligations are met correctly and on time, working with external advisors where needed.

 - Manage and develop direct reports — driving, motivating, and growing them into a team that works hard and delivers.

 - Handle company, legal, and HR administration — for example, working with lawyers to incorporate entities and set up bank accounts in new jurisdictions, and managing payroll across markets.




WHO YOU ARE

 - A qualified accountant (CPA / ACCA or equivalent) with at least five years of experience, including managing or mentoring others.

 - Someone genuinely diligent about accuracy — unreconciled accounts and late collections bother you.

 - Someone who can drive, motivate, and develop a team — getting people to work hard and put in the hours, while building them up rather than burning them out.

 - Willing to put in the hours to succeed. This is a role for someone who wants to build a career in a fast-growing startup, takes on more to grow, and isn't looking for a nine-to-five seat to warm.

 - A prolific user of AI — you reach for it daily to work faster and sharper, without ever compromising accuracy, and you build with it rather than just talk about it.

 - Organized across many moving parts — you can run finance and administrative responsibilities without dropping either.




WHY THIS ROLE?

You'll own a critical function in a fast-scaling company, work closely with the founders and finance leadership, an

source_jobs.title: Managing Director
source_jobs.description:
About the Company

Imara Talent Global is a mission-driven company headquartered in the United States with primary operations in Ghana. Our mission is to create meaningful work opportunities that enable Africans to achieve an elevated standard of living while delivering exceptional support to companies worldwide. We are building a bridge between exceptional Ghanaian accounting talent and the US market. Our first cohort of 10 accountants will launch in 2026, growing to 75+ by 2027 and beyond. This is an opportunity to be a founding leader of something transformative.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a dynamic, entrepreneurial Managing Director to lead the launch and growth of our inaugural office in Accra, Ghana. This is a rare founding leadership role: you will build the team, establish the culture, shape the infrastructure, and represent Imara Talent in the Ghanaian market. The right candidate is a natural leader with deep roots in the Ghanaian professional community, a strong accounting background, and operational drive to build from the ground up. As we grow, so will your role.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 1. Recruiting & Team Building
    
    - Lead recruitment of the inaugural cohort of 10 accountants in 2026, prioritizing candidates with strong technical skills and professional development potential
    
    - Hire and manage administrative support staff to sustain day-to-day operations
    
    - Scale the team to 75+ accountants by 2027 and participate in long-range workforce planning

 2. Team Management & Development
    
    - Provide direct management, coaching, and mentorship to the accounting team and administrative staff
    
    - Foster a culture of excellence, integrity, accountability, and continuous growth
    
    - Partner with the US team to design and deliver onboarding, technical training, and professional development
    
    - Ensure high levels of work product and quality control across the team to build trust with clients
    
    - Monitor team performance and implement feedback mechanisms and career pathways

 3. Partnerships & Community Engagement
    
    - Build and maintain relationships with local academic institutions (universities, polytechnics, professional associations) to support talent pipelines
    
    - Represent Imara Talent Global at industry events, career fairs, and community forums
    
    - Cultivate strategic local partnerships that enhance the company’s reputation and reach in Ghana

 4. Executive Partnership
    
    - Serve as the primary liaison between the Ghana office and US executive leadership
    
    - Provide regular operational, financial, and people-related reporting to HQ
    
    - Act as the face of Imara Talent in Ghana, modeling our values and mission publicly and internally

 5. Office Launch & Operations
    
    - Identify, negotiate, and secure suitable office space in Accra, aligned with operational and cultural needs
    
    - Procure and manage technology, equipment, and tools required for a remote-capable team
    
    - Establish office policies, workflows, and operational standards in coordination with US leadership




WHAT YOU HAVE

Required

 - Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field

 - 5+ years of professional experience in accounting or finance

 - 3+ years of management experience, ideally in a startup, NGO, or multi-stakeholder environment

 - Demonstrated experience in recruiting, hiring, and developing professional teams

 - Excellent interpersonal, communication, and relationship-building skills

 - Strong organizational and project management capabilities

 - Deep familiarity with the Ghanaian professional landscape, culture, and business environment

Preferred

 - Professional accounting certification (ICAG, ACCA, CPA, or equivalent)

 - Experience working with or for US-based organizations or in cross-cultural professional settings

 - Existing network within academic institutions, professi

source_jobs.title: Head of Operations (Device Financing)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Mogo is dedicated to fostering upward social mobility in diverse communities by providing credit access to entrepreneurs who are often excluded from traditional banking. Through a combination of asset financing, fintech innovations, AI-based underwriting, and proprietary car portals, we empower local entrepreneurs, such as motorcycle taxi riders, Uber/Bolt drivers, and small business owners, to purchase their own vehicles and obtain loans to expand their businesses.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

Mogo Tanzania is expanding into smartphone and device financing and is seeking a strategic, execution-driven Head of Operations to lead and scale our operational function. This role will be responsible for building operational processes, ensuring seamless execution across sales, dealer management, customer care, and debt collection, and preparing the business for rapid growth.

The Head of Operations will play a key role in ensuring customer success, strong debt collection practices, and operational excellence, driving both efficiency and quality across the business.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 1. Strategy & Alignment
    
    - Define and implement an operational strategy to support the launch and scale-up of device financing.
    
    - Work closely with the Head of Device Financing and other leadership to align operational strategy with business goals.

 2. Process & Risk Management
    
    - Drive continuous process improvements to maximize efficiency and minimize risk.
    
    - Partner with Risk team to enforce robust fraud prevention and customer verification processes.
    
    - Ensure all processes comply with company policies and local regulations.
    
    - Oversee the management of returned and repossessed phones to recover value and minimize losses.

 3. Customer Experience & Performance
    
    - Ensure customer experience is positive throughout the financing journey, from onboarding to repayment.
    
    - Oversee and strengthen debt collection processes, working closely with Mogo’s existing DC team to minimize defaults.
    
    - Monitor KPIs related to customer satisfaction, churn, repayment rates, and default management.

 4. Team Leadership & Development
    
    - Manage and scale the operations team.
    
    - Build frameworks for training and onboarding to ensure rapid productivity of new hires.
    
    - Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, and performance excellence.




WHAT YOU HAVE

Must-Haves (Required)

 - 3-5 years of experience in fintech or related industries, with strong exposure to operational excellence.

 - Demonstrated experience leading teams and delivering strong performance outcomes.

 - Proven track record in scaling operations in fast-growth environments.

 - Experience working with dealers, distributors, or large partner networks.

 - Strong leadership and people management skills; experience managing mid-level managers and large teams.

 - Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.

 - Ability to balance strategic vision with hands-on execution in a startup/scale-up environment.

 - Strong stakeholder management and cross-functional collaboration skills.

Nice to have (Preferred)

 - Experience in device financing, mobility, or e-commerce operations.

 - Familiarity with the Tanzania smartphone and retail ecosystem.

 - Prior experience with process digitization and CRM/ERP systems.

 - Fluency in additional local languages.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive monthly salary and results-oriented bonus system

 - Housing allowance and periodic flights back to your home country.

 - Relocation support (flights, visas)

 - Health insurance

source_jobs.title: Product Lead, MSME Loans
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Mogo Kenya is a CBK–licensed lender that started operations in Kenya in 2019. Since then, we have disbursed 40B KES across Kenya, and this year celebrated financing 500,000+ smartphones across East Africa. We are a fast-growing and ambitious organization, sitting within the Eleving Group, a global fintech operating consumer lending across 17 countries. In East Africa, Mogo provides asset-backed products to underserved customers across Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, and Zambia.



We are launching a new business-focused lending product for MSME owners and are looking for a Product Lead to own it as we scale the product.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This is a hands-on, cross-functional role for someone who can take a financial product from pilot to live, fully scaled operations. You will own the scaling of the product – finalizing product design and defining the go-to-market plan, as well as taking full ownership of product P&L once scaled. You'll work daily with technology, operations, and credit/risk, and you'll be the person accountable for the product landing well.



The MSME product has full support from senior stakeholders and the relevant legal approval, as well as an existing tech foundation and Mogo network of 90+ branches to scale from – we are looking for someone to utilize this strong base to ensure a successful product which can be expanded into a fully operational product line across Kenya.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 1. Product & proposition
    
    - Finalize design for a fully scalable product from customer acquisition (sales app design, loan officer processes, underwriting decisions) to subsequent loan issuances (USSD menus, app design, customer eligibility and pricing).
    
    - Iterate on and document pilots to inform product decisions.

 2. Scale & operations
    
    - Own the operations and scaling of the MSME loan product and document it clearly for credit, operations, and frontline teams.
    
    - Take ownership of live product pilots, and expand them - broadening eligibility criteria, segments, and geography as the data supports.
    
    - Own the go-to-market: define the launch and rollout strategy across the branch network and digital channels, including positioning, pricing communication, and the customer acquisition approach.
    
    - Take ownership of the product P&L and daily operations once scaled.

 3. Cross-functional delivery
    
    - Act as the bridge between product, technology, risk/credit, and the loan officer team.
    
    - Manage internal partners and hold them to delivery commitments.

 4. Market & compliance
    
    - Maintain a clear view of the competitive landscape (other MSME and digital lenders) and feed it into the proposition.
    
    - Ensure the product meets CBK requirements, including interest-rate and cost disclosure obligations for digital lending.




WHAT YOU HAVE

Must-Haves (Required)

 - 3+ years in product management, business development, or a closely related role, ideally in fintech, banking, or digital lending at a start-up or fast-growing company.

 - Comfort working with data to define and track KPIs and to make decisions under uncertainty.

 - Proven ability to work across technical and non-technical teams and to manage external partners.

 - Excellent communication - able to translate complex concepts into clear documentation for relevant stakeholders.

Preferred

 - Experience in the Kenyan or East African fintech market, or relevant emerging markets experience.

 - Strong grasp of consumer or MSME lending fundamentals: underwriting, unit economics, cost of funds, portfolio quality, and collections.

 - Familiarity with mobile-money rails (M-Pesa) and USSD product design

 - Experience scaling a product from pilot to full operation.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE (FIRST 6 MONTHS)

 - The MSME loan product is live and fully functioning.

 - The pilot has been measured, and either expanded or refined based on clear evidence.

 - A reporting framework

source_jobs.title: Product Owner, Financing Products
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Mogo Kenya is revolutionizing financial access. By offering a dynamic suite of products that includes boda-boda financing, car logbook loans, and smartphone loans, we leverage a nationwide network of 90+ branches to put capital into the hands of traditionally underserved communities. Backed by Eleving Group, a global fintech spanning Europe, Africa, and Asia we are on a mission to drive sustainable financial inclusion.

 

This is an exciting time to join. We move fast, think big, and give real ownership to the people who drive our growth. If you want to build and scale financial products that change lives, this is the place to do it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Mogo Kenya is expanding its financing products — including boda-boda, logbook, and smartphone loans — across a growing network of 90+ branches and digital channels. As we continue to scale, we're looking for ambitious, execution-driven Product Owners to take ownership of products that directly impact customer growth and business performance.

This is a hands-on role where you'll own the full lifecycle of a lending product, from shaping its design, pricing, eligibility criteria, customer journey, and unit economics to driving go-to-market execution through both digital marketing and in-branch campaigns. You'll work closely with cross-functional teams to continuously test, optimize, and improve product performance based on customer insights and commercial results.

Designed as a fast-track career opportunity, this role is ideal for high-potential individuals who thrive in fast-paced environments, enjoy solving commercial problems, and want to build leadership experience early. Success is measured by your ability to execute, learn quickly, and deliver tangible business outcomes, with a clear pathway into product leadership and management.

Reporting directly to the Country Director, you'll have significant ownership from day one and the opportunity to help shape products used by thousands of customers across Kenya.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

1. Product Ownership & Development

 - Own the product end to end. It's design, pricing, terms, eligibility, customer journey and overall economics.

 - Continuously analyze customer needs and market data to create and refine the product so it stays competitive and relevant.

 - Define and prioritize the product roadmap; translate business goals into clear requirements for delivery teams.

 - Identify and launch new business lines, segments and product variations to open additional growth.

2. Growth & Scaling Issuances

 - Take full responsibility for scaling the product growing issuance volumes and portfolio within agreed budget and risk appetite.

 - Build and develop new client-acquisition channels (digital, branch, partner, agent and referral).

 - Drive both new client acquisition and up-sale / cross-sell to existing customers.

 - Improve the end-to-end sales funnel and conversion, removing friction across the customer journey.

3. Marketing & Communication

 - Own the full marketing plan for the product, from strategy through to execution and results.

 - Develop and oversee all content across channels - digital, social, print and offline / in-branch.

 - Initiate campaigns, promotions and special offers to keep issuances on plan and respond to market conditions.

 - Manage the marketing budget for the product and optimize spend against acquisition cost and return.

4. Cross-functional Leadership & Process Improvement

 - Lead IT and digital projects that improve product presentation, the sales funnel and operational efficiency.

 - Work closely with risk, credit, operations, sales and finance to balance growth with portfolio quality.

 - Generate and implement ideas to improve branches, shops and other customer touchpoints.

5. Performance & Accountability

 - Define, track and act on KPIs for product growth, customer acquisition, conversion, retention and profitability.

 - Use data and customer insight to make decisio

source_jobs.title: Financial Controller, Tanzania
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Mogo's mission is to enable upward social mobility for people in diverse communities around the world by providing entrepreneurs who are excluded from traditional banking systems with access to credit. By combining asset financing, fintech, AI-based underwriting, and proprietary car portals, we enable these local entrepreneurs (motorcycle taxi riders, Uber/Bolt drivers, and small business owners) to find and purchase their own vehicles.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a Financial Controller to help achieve regional financial efficiency by building and owning the Tanzania country finance function, largely from scratch.

The role will own monthly P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow reporting, stand up robust accounting procedures, manage auditors and tax authorities, and integrate the country into Eleving Group’s financial ecosystem.

 


WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Establish robust accounting and financial procedures and processes to ensure effective financial and accounting reporting.

 - Own monthly P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow reporting, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and compliance.

 - Partner with local and regional stakeholders, providing financial insights and analysis to guide commercial and operational decision-making.

 - Manage relationships with auditors, tax authorities, and other financial partners.

 - Collaborate closely with the regional HQ (finance team) to ensure smooth integration into Eleving Group’s financial ecosystem.

 - Lead initiatives to drive accounting and financial improvements.

 - Monitor adherence to local regulations and international accounting standards, proactively mitigating financial risks.

 


WHAT YOU HAVE

 - Higher education in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or Business Administration

 - Previous experience in the fields of finance, business administration, project management, and similar

 - Understanding of IFRS accounting principles (B/S, P/L, CF)

 - Advanced Excel skills and familiarity with financial software and databases

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

 - Attention to detail with a commitment to accuracy

 - Ability to manage multiple projects and meet tight deadlines

 - Proficiency in English

 - Can-do attitude and ownership mindset



WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary and results-oriented bonus system

 - Personal and professional development

 - Monthly housing allowance

 - Relocation support (flights, visas, vaccination )

source_jobs.title: Financial Controller, Uganda
source_jobs.description:

Mogo's mission is to enable upward social mobility for people in diverse communities around the world by providing entrepreneurs who are excluded from traditional banking systems with access to credit. By combining asset financing, fintech, AI-based underwriting, and proprietary car portals, we enable these local entrepreneurs (motorcycle taxi riders, Uber/Bolt drivers, and small business owners) to find and purchase their own vehicles.

notes: Multiple companies posting on this board, including Paystack, Mogo, and AltSchool Africa. Various roles in fintech, education, and more.
